seoTitle: How to Fix PayPal 'Can't Transfer Your Money' Error (2026)
metaDescription: Troubleshoot PayPal transfer errors by checking your linked bank details, website payment preferences, and Resolution Center for holds.
intro: Seeing an error like "We can't complete this transaction right now" when trying to withdraw funds is frustrating, especially when your money is otherwise available. This guide covers the most common causes and fixes, from bank data mismatches to encrypted payment settings. It's for anyone stuck trying to move their PayPal balance out.

steps.heading: Check for account notifications
steps.body: Look at the notification bell for any active restrictions that might be blocking your transfer.

steps.heading: Review your linked bank details
steps.body: Go to Wallet and click your linked bank account to confirm the stored bank name matches your legal account identity.

steps.heading: Update or re-add your bank account
steps.body: If the details look wrong, remove the bank and re-enter the correct routing and account information.

steps.heading: Check website payment preferences
steps.body: Go to Settings, then Website Payments, and toggle off Encrypted Website Payments if it's causing conflicts.

steps.heading: Try an alternative transfer method
steps.body: Return to Transfer Money and try an instant card destination instead of a standard bank route, then confirm the transfer.

faq.question: Why does PayPal randomly block a transfer?
faq.answer: PayPal runs real-time risk checks on high-volume or cross-border transfers, which can trigger a temporary block.

faq.question: Should I check the Resolution Center too?
faq.answer: Yes, it's worth confirming there are no active compliance flags restricting your wallet before trying again.

faq.question: Is switching to instant card transfer faster?
faq.answer: Instant transfers to a debit card often process within minutes, compared to a few business days for standard bank transfers.

faq.question: Does an instant transfer cost extra?
faq.answer: Instant transfers typically carry a small percentage fee compared to free standard bank transfers.
